intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Catabrosella araratica." However, I found that Catabrosella araratica is a plant in the family Asteraceae. Based on my search, I found that the closest information is about the plant Catabrosella araratica is likely to be an outdated name for a plant that has now been identified as a synonym of the plant Asteriscus araraticus (also known as "Catabrosella araratica" is an older name). 

Here is a paragraph based on the available information about Asteriscus araraticus:

Asteriscus araraticus, previously known as Catabrosella araratica, is an annual or biennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant Asteriscus araraticus has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, and its consumption is associated with several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Asteriscus araraticus include essential oils, flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat digestive issues and other health conditions.
